mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that increases consumer confidence and disposable income, driving equity valuations higher over the subsequent 20 trading days through elevated earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:October 2016 payroll level at 144,952 thousand persons
regime_hint: post-crisis expansion with steady labor market recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or months with PAYEMS growth > 150k do not produce significantly higher 20-day forward returns than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.5% per annum, equity risk premiums compress as the opportunity cost of equity capital declines relative to near-zero cash returns, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as portfolio rebalancing flows into equities.
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy regime
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window during periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals synchronized demand expansion across residential and consumer sectors, indicating broad-based economic acceleration that drives equity returns higher over 20 trading days through elevated corporate earnings growth.
evidence_refs: fact:September 2016 HOUST at 1047 thousand units, October 2016 RSAFS at 465,914 million dollars
failure_condition: Rank IC for the interaction term < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or instances where both HOUST and RSAFS increase simultaneously do not produce significantly higher 20-day returns than single-series increases (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the industrial production index (INDPRO) below 104 signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk, causing equity investors to reduce growth expectations and demand higher risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as portfolio rebalancing accelerates.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window when INDPRO < 104.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0% while payroll growth remains positive, this signals a decelerating but still-functioning labor market that creates uncertainty about earnings sustainability. Equity investors respond by reducing valuations over the subsequent 60 trading days as forward guidance becomes more cautious.
evidence_refs: fact:October 2016 UNRATE at 4.9%, PAYEMS at 144,952 thousand persons
failure_condition: Rank IC for the interaction term > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or instances meeting both trigger conditions do not produce significantly lower 60-day returns than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) above 242 signals inflation acceleration that forces the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, raising real discount rates and compressing equity valuations over 20 trading days as market participants anticipate future rate hikes.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day forward returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window when CPIAUCSL > 242.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured on a quarterly basis above 16,700 billion chained 2017 dollars indicates sustained economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth and equity valuations. This always-on trend signal produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as long as the economy remains in expansion mode.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2016 GDPC1 at 16,702.1 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or quarters with GDPC1 > 16,700 do not produce significantly higher 60-day forward returns than contraction periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates while the unemployment rate simultaneously declines below 4.9%, this signals a tight labor market coupled with strong productive capacity utilization. This interaction indicates an overheating economy that will force monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity investors price in future rate increases.
evidence_refs: fact:September 2016 INDPRO at 104.226, October 2016 UNRATE at 4.9%
regime_hint: late-cycle overheating with tight labor markets
failure_condition: Rank IC for the interaction term > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or instances meeting both trigger conditions do not produce significantly lower 20-day returns than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
